messages.content:
You are drafting one section of a formal business report for Northwind Trading Co.. Write in the report's dialect exactly as specified below. Ground every claim in the sources bound to this section; never invent figures.

Dialect contract: write plain paragraphs separated by blank lines; NO markdown headings/bold/lists; tables as GitHub markdown tables; every figure sourced from data must be written as [[numeral|familyId|locator]] — the first field is the exact text the reader sees, so it must be the actual number (e.g. [[220,500|fam_ab12|sum(fam_ar_transactions.amount)]]), never the word "figure" or any other placeholder; locator is agg(tableName.column) — agg one of sum|avg|min|max|count, tableName one of the warehouse tables shown in this section's data (each belongs to the family you cite) — adding a row filter when the figure covers only matching rows, e.g. sum(fam_ar_transactions.amount where channel=search); for document sources, a short quote reference; only cite familyIds bound to this section.

Tools: use ask_user only for genuine ambiguity in the data or its business framing — never about the dialect, citation markers, or locator grammar, which the reader never sees; resolve mechanics yourself and raise_flag if precision suffers. Use raise_flag for judgment calls the rules mark as needing review.

Global rules for this report:
- Round all currency to whole dollars.
- Write in the past tense.

Standing guidance for this project (applies to every document in this project — follow unless blueprint guidance or a section instruction contradicts it):
- Northwind's fiscal year starts in April.

Standing guidance for this blueprint (learned from the builder and past runs — follow unless a section instruction contradicts it):
- Always express spend deltas in percentages.

Section 2: Revenue Analysis
Instruction: Analyze revenue by channel and call out the largest mover.

Sources bound to this section:
fam_marketing_spend (periodic, quarter 2026-Q2)
schema: date TEXT, channel TEXT, amount REAL
sum(amount)=190900 across 12 rows

Rules for this section:
- [style] Lead with the total before the breakdown.
- [assert] Total spend equals the sum of channel spend. (sql: SELECT sum(amount) FROM fam_marketing_spend WHERE _period = :period)
(assert rules are verified deterministically after drafting; style rules shape your writing; judgment rules should prompt raise_flag when triggered.)

Sections already written (for continuity — do not repeat their content):
Executive Summary
Revenue rose across every paid channel this quarter.

User steers:
1. Lead with the revenue headline.

Answered questions:
Q: Which channels count as paid?
A: Search and social only.

Write the section now.

function.description: Ask the user a blocking-with-fallback question when the source data or its business framing is genuinely ambiguous. Never ask about report mechanics (the dialect, citation markers, locator grammar) — the user never sees those; resolve them yourself and raise_flag if precision suffers. Continue drafting using the fallback unless an answer arrives.
function.name: ask_user

function.description: Flag a passage that needs the user's judgment before the report can be released. Does not stop drafting.
function.name: raise_flag
